Nearly three fourth (75%) of women in India are anemic, with the prevalence 5of moderate to severe anemia being highest (50%) among pregnant women . It is 6estimated that nutritional anemia contributes to about 24% of maternal deaths every year and is one of the important causes of low birth weight. It adversely affects work output among adults and learning ability in children. Iodine deficiency disorders (IDD) are very common among large sections of population in several parts of the country. About 167 million are estimated to be living in IDD endemic areas. Iodine deficiency causes goiter
